name: cron
title: cron
description:
process scheduling daemon
The cron daemon is a background process that runs particular programs at
particular times (for example, every minute, day, week, or month), as
specified in a crontab. By default, users may also create crontabs of
their own so that processes are run on their behalf.
.
Output from the commands is usually mailed to the system administrator
(or to the user in question); you should probably install a mail system
as well so that you can receive these messages.
.
This cron package does not provide any system maintenance tasks. Basic
periodic maintenance tasks are provided by other packages, such
as checksecurity.

commands.name: cron
commands.description: daemon to execute scheduled commands (Vixie Cron)

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use crontab because process scheduling daemon.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use crontab as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `crontab [-h]` as documented upstream for crontab.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `crontab [-u user] [-n] file` as documented upstream for crontab.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `crontab [-u user] [-i] {-e | -l | -r}` as documented upstream for crontab.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair crontab with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `crontab --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install cron. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `crontab [-u user] [-i] {-e | -l | -r}`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: process scheduling daemon.
